title: CLAUDE.md
text: SpaNorm Bioconductor R package spatially-aware library-size normalisation spatial transcriptomics data. fits gene-wise negative binomial (NB) regression whose covariates spatially-smooth thin-plate-spline (TPS) functions, decomposes variation library-size (technical) vs. library-size-independent (biological) components, produces adjusted data (default: log percentile- adjusted counts, “logpac”).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: pure-R package compiled code (src/ empty). Run package root. Prefer deploy-bioc skill full pre-push ritual (document → vignette → review → R CMD check → BiocCheck → version bump → commit/push). CI (.github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml) runs R CMD check macOS/Windows/Ubuntu (release + devel).
code: # Build & install locally devtools::document()          # regenerate NAMESPACE + man/*.Rd from roxygen (roxygen2 v8.0.0) devtools::load_all()          # load without installing (fastest dev loop) devtools::install()  # Tests (testthat 3e) devtools::test()                                    # all tests testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-mainSpaNorm.R")   # single file devtools::test(filter = "fitSpaNormNB")             # files matching test-<filter>.R  # Checks (must pass before a Bioconductor push) devtools::check()             # R CMD check BiocCheck::BiocCheck()        # Bioconductor-specific checks  # Vignette / docs devtools::build_vignettes() pkgdown::build_site()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: entry points S4 generics methods SpatialExperiment Seurat objects (SpatialFeatureExperiment). container-specific methods extract counts/coords/size factors, delegate shared internal implementation, write results back container: SpaNorm() — fit model write adjusted values (logcounts assay / Seurat data layer). SpaNormSVG() — spatially-variable-gene calling via likelihood-ratio test (full vs. biology-free nested model). SpaNormPCA() — GLM-PCA approximation Pearson/deviance residuals null (LS + batch + gene mean) model. filterGenes(), fastSizeFactors(), plotSpatial(), plotCovariate(), topSVGs().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: SpaNorm() method → .spaNormCore() (shared SPE + Seurat methods) → decides cache-reuse vs. refit → fitSpaNorm() → fitSpaNormNB() (R/fitSpaNormNB.R, IRLS engine). fitSpaNorm() builds design matrix W : logLS, biology TPS basis, logLS × LS-TPS interaction, optional batch columns. column tagged wtype "biology", "ls", "batch" — labelling lets adjustment/SVG/PCA steps selectively zero-technical variation. TPS bases built bs.tps() (tensor product splines::ns along scaled x/y). df.tps may differ biology vs. LS; rectangular tissue per-axis df derived aspect ratio. Size factors: absent, computed internally via scran::quickCluster + calculateSumFactors. result SpaNormFit S4 object (R/AllClasses.R) $ accessor setValidity invariant. stores W, coefficients alpha, gmean, dispersion psi, wtype, loglik, sampling.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: SpaNormFit stored metadata(spe)$SpaNorm (SPE) spe@misc$SpaNorm (Seurat) reused subsequent calls unless overwrite=TRUE fit-defining parameter changed (df.tps, lambda., batch, gene.model, dimensions) — see guard .spaNormCore() matchDftps/matchLambda. SpaNormSVG/SpaNormPCA retrieve cached fit (via getSpaNormFit) rather refitting.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: getAdjustmentFun() maps adj.method → one normaliseLogPAC (default/“auto”), normalisePearson, normaliseMedianBio, normaliseMeanBio. row-wise (per-gene) transforms. normaliseBlocked() applies chosen transform contiguous gene-blocks rbinds results — identical whole-matrix result. Blocking engages either: - input counts DelayedArray (disk-backed, e.g. HDF5Array) → finite block.size keeps array --core (block size follows DelayedArray::getAutoBlockSize()), - multi-worker BPPARAM supplied → blocks run parallel via BiocParallel::bplapply. -memory + serial takes direct whole-matrix path (block.size = Inf). dispersion-winsorisation threshold global statistic, subsetFitGenes() carries psi.max attribute per block.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Optional acceleration via torch package (TensorFlow — migrated 1.8.0; reticulate/Python). backend = "auto"|"cpu"|"gpu" SpaNorm/SpaNormSVG. Device resolved per session cached .spanorm_env environment (resetGPUCache() clears , used tests): - Device precedence: CUDA → MPS (Apple Silicon) → CPU (getBackendDevice). - dtype differs device: CUDA/CPU use float64 exact CPU parity; MPS float64 uses float32 (getBackendDtype). Expect small numerical differences MPS. - IRLS math written *_gpu helpers (tcrossprod_gpu, dnbinom_gpu, colSums_gpu, …) operate either base-R matrices torch_tensors. Watch mixing base-R tensor objects — use toGPUMatrix/toRMatrix/is_torch_tensor boundaries. dnbinom_gpu/pnbinom/qnbinom real torch implementations NB density.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: S4 argument flow: extra fitting parameters (e.g. maxn.psi, step.factor, maxit.*) declared SpaNorm generic ride ... unpacked consumed (fitSpaNormNB). adding tuning parameter, thread generic signature ... forwarding .spaNormCore() → fitSpaNorm() → fitSpaNormNB(), every intermediate signature. single-sample SpatialExperiment objects supported (checkSPE errors multiple sample_ids). counts must non-negative integers (checkSPE/checkSeurat enforce ). ggplot NSE symbols used aes() declared R/zzz.R via utils::globalVariables() silence R CMD check “undefined global” NOTEs — add new plotting symbols . Example dataset: data(HumanDLPFC) — small filtered Visium SpatialExperiment used across man examples, vignette, parity tests.

title: SpaNorm: Spatially aware library size normalisation
text: SpaNorm spatially aware library size normalisation method removes library size effects, retaining biology. Library sizes need removed molecular datasets allow comparisons across observations, case, across space. Bhuva et al. (Bhuva et al. 2024) Atta et al. (Atta et al. 2024) shown standard single-cell inspired library size normalisation approaches appropriate spatial molecular datasets often remove biological signals . library size confounds biology spatial molecular data. SpaNorm uses unique approach spatially constraint modelling approach model gene expression (e.g., counts) remove library size effects, retaining biology. achieves three key innovations: Optmial decomposition spatial variation spatially smooth library size associated (technical) library size independent (biology) variation using generalized linear models (GLMs). Computing spatially smooth functions (using thin plate splines) represent gene- location-/cell-/spot- specific size factors. Adjustment data using percentile adjusted counts (PAC) (Salim et al. 2022), well adjustment approaches (e.g., Pearson). SpaNorm package can installed follows:
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  # release version BiocManager::install("SpaNorm") # development version from GitHub BiocManager::install("bhuvad/SpaNorm")

title: SpaNorm: Spatially aware library size normalisation
text: begin loading example 10x Visium data profiling dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC) human brain. data ~4,000 spots covers genome-wide measurements. example data filtered remove lowly expressed genes (using filterGenes(HumanDLPFC, prop = 0.1)). filtering retains genes expressed least 10% cells.  filterGenes function returns logical vector indicating genes kept. log-transformed raw counts visualised gene MOBP marker oligodendrocytes enriched white matter (WM) (Maynard et al. 2021). Despite marker region, see fact absent white matter region.

MT-CYB AC007325.4 #> rowData names(2): gene_name gene_biotype #> colnames(4015): AAACAAGTATCTCCCA-1 AAACACCAATAACTGC-1 ... #>   TTGTTTCCATACAACT-1 TTGTTTGTGTAAATTC-1 #> colData names(3): cell_count sample_id AnnotatedCluster #> reducedDimNames(0): #> mainExpName: NULL #> altExpNames(0): #> spatialCoords names(2) : pxl_col_in_fullres pxl_row_in_fullres #> imgData names(4): sample_id image_id data scaleFactor  # plot regions p_region = plotSpatial(HumanDLPFC, colour = AnnotatedCluster, size = 0.5) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Paired", guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 15, size = 5))) +   ggtitle("Region") p_region # filter genes expressed in 20% of spots keep = filterGenes(HumanDLPFC, 0.2) table(keep) #> keep #> FALSE  TRUE  #>  2568  2508 # subset genes HumanDLPFC = HumanDLPFC[keep, ] logcounts(HumanDLPFC) = log2(counts(HumanDLPFC) + 1)  p_counts = plotSpatial(     HumanDLPFC,     colour = MOBP,     what = "expression",     assay = "logcounts",     size = 0.5   ) +   scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "F") +   ggtitle("logCounts") p_region + p_counts

title: SpaNorm: Spatially aware library size normalisation
text: SpaNorm normalises data two steps: (1) fitting SpaNorm model library sizes; (2) adjusting data using fit model. single call SpaNorm() function enough run two steps. speed computation, model fit using smaller proportion spots/cells (default 0.25). can modified using sample.p parameter. output (can switched setting verbose = FALSE), shows two steps normalisation. model fitting step, 1004 cells/spots used fit negative binomial (NB) model. Subsequent output shows fit performed alternating estimation dispersion parameter estimation NB parameters fixing dispersion. output also shows intermediate fit converges, final fit. accuracy fit can controlled modifying tolerance parameter tol (default 1e-4). Next, data adjusted using fit model. following approaches implemented count data: adj.method = "logpac" (default) - percentile adjusted counts (PAC) estimates count gene location/spot/cell using model contain unwanted effects library size. adj.method = "person" - Pearson residuals factoring unwanted effects. adj.method = "meanbio" - mean gene location estimated biological component model. adj.method = "medbio" - median gene location estimated biological component model. data stored logcounts assay SpatialExperiment object. normalisation, see MOBP enriched white matter.

title: SpaNorm: Spatially aware library size normalisation
text: large datasets, normalisation step can parallelised across workers using BPPARAM argument, accepts BiocParallelParam object BiocParallel package. Normalisation runs serially default (SerialParam()). counts assay DelayedArray (example, disk-backed HDF5Array), SpaNorm automatically normalises data blocks full matrix never loaded memory . detected data needs additional arguments, results identical -memory path. combining two, prefer BiocParallel::SnowParam() MulticoreParam() disk-backed (e.g. HDF5-backed) DelayedArray: fork-based workers (MulticoreParam, default Linux/macOS) inherit copy open file handle, can cause HDF5Array reads concurrent forked workers fail return incorrect data. using optional GPU backend (backend = "gpu" "auto", requires torch package CUDA Apple Silicon/Metal device), SpaNorm automatically detects available accelerator memory , datasets large fit -device , fits model gene-blocks peak GPU memory stays bounded. detected automatically needs additional arguments, results match CPU path within small numerical tolerance floating-point summation order. detected memory budget can overridden gpu.mem.budget (bytes) needed.
code: library(BiocParallel) # parallelise normalisation across 4 workers HumanDLPFC = SpaNorm(HumanDLPFC, BPPARAM = MulticoreParam(workers = 4)) library(HDF5Array) # wrap the counts as a disk-backed DelayedArray counts(HumanDLPFC) = writeHDF5Array(counts(HumanDLPFC)) # normalisation now runs block-wise, bounding peak memory HumanDLPFC = SpaNorm(HumanDLPFC) # override the auto-detected GPU memory budget (bytes) if needed HumanDLPFC = SpaNorm(HumanDLPFC, backend = "gpu", gpu.mem.budget = 4 * 1024^3)

title: SpaNorm: Spatially aware library size normalisation
text: SpaNorm’s underlying negative binomial fitting engine also available standalone, general-purpose per-gene GLM fitter via fitNB(). useful packages want SpaNorm’s IRLS fitting machinery (per-gene dispersion estimation, ridge regularisation, robust winsorisation) design matrix, rather SpaNorm’s spatial (library-size/biology) model. Unlike SpaNorm(), fitNB() fits log(mu) = W %*% t(alpha) directly, built-intercept – include column ones W per-gene baseline required.
code: set.seed(1) Y <- matrix(rpois(20 * 50, 5), 20, 50) W <- cbind(intercept = 1, covariate = scale(seq_len(50))[, 1]) fit <- fitNB(Y, W, verbose = FALSE) str(fit$alpha) #>  num [1:20, 1:2] 1.65 1.6 1.72 1.62 1.62 ...

title: Spatially-aware normalisation for spatial transcriptomics data
text: SpaNorm spatially aware library size normalisation method removes library size effects, retaining biology. Library sizes need removed molecular datasets allow comparisons across observations, case, across space. Bhuva et al. (2024) Atta et al. (2023) shown standard single-cell inspired library size normalisation approaches appropriate spatial molecular datasets often remove biological signals . library size confounds biology spatial molecular data. SpaNorm uses unique approach spatially constraint modelling approach model gene expression (e.g., counts) remove library size effects, retaining biology. achieves three key innovations: Computing spatially smooth functions (using thin plate splines) represent gene- location-/cell-/spot- specific size factors. Optimal decomposition spatial variation spatially smooth library size associated (technical) library size independent (biology) variation using generalized linear models (GLMs). Adjustment data using percentile adjusted counts (PAC) (Salim et al., 2022), well adjustment approaches (e.g., Pearson).

text: Performs normalisation spatial transcriptomics data using spatially-dependent spot- gene- specific size factors.

title: Spatially-dependent normalisation for spatial transcriptomics data — SpaNorm

title: Spatially-dependent normalisation for spatial transcriptomics data — SpaNorm
text: spe SpatialExperiment Seurat object, count data stored 'counts' 'data' assays respectively. sample.p numeric, specifying (maximum) proportion cells/spots sample model fitting (default 0.25). gene.model character, specifying model use gene/protein abundances (default 'nb'). 'nb' count based datasets. adj.method character, specifying method use adjust data (default 'auto', see details) scale.factor numeric, specifying sample-specific scaling factor scale adjusted count. df.tps numeric, length 1 2, specifying maximum degrees freedom thin-plate spline biology library size effects, respectively (default 6, see details). lambda.numeric, length 1 2, specifying smoothing parameter regularizing regression coefficients (default 0.0001, see details). Actual lambda.used lambda.* ncol(spe). batch vector numeric matrix, specifying batch design regress (default NULL, representing batch effects). See details information define variable. tol numeric, specifying tolerance convergence (default 1e-4). step.factor numeric, specifying multiplicative factor decrease IRLS step log-likelihood diverges (default 0.5). maxit.nb numeric, specifying maximum number IRLS iteration estimating NB mean parameters given dispersion parameter (default 50). maxit.psi numeric, specifying maximum number IRLS iterations estimate dispersion parameter (default 25). maxn.psi numeric, specifying maximum number cells/spots sample dispersion estimation (default 500). overwrite logical, specifying whether force recomputation overwrite existing fit (default FALSE). Note df.tps, batch, lambda., gene.model changed, model recomputed overwritten. backend character, specifying backend use computations (default 'auto', see details). 'gpu', GPU-based computations used available, otherwise CPU-based computations used. BPPARAM BiocParallelParam object specifying parallelise normalisation step gene-blocks (default BiocParallel::SerialParam(), .e. parallelisation). Pass e.g. BiocParallel::MulticoreParam() speed logpac transform large datasets. verbose logical, specifying whether show update messages (default TRUE). assay character, specifying assay use Seurat objects (default NULL uses object's default assay). SpaNorm models raw counts, set assay holding raw counts (e.g. 'Spatial') default assay transformed one 'SCT'. Ignored SpatialExperiment objects. ... parameters fitting parameters.

title: Spatially-dependent normalisation for spatial transcriptomics data — SpaNorm
text: SpaNorm works first fitting spatial regression model library size data. Normalised data can computed using various adjustment approaches. negative binomial gene-model used, data can adjusted using following approaches: 'logpac', 'pearson', 'medbio', 'meanbio'. df.tps parameter specifies degrees freedom thin-plate spline. 1 value provided, specifies degrees freedom biology degrees freedom library size half . 2 values provided, first value specifies degrees freedom biology second value specifies degrees freedom library size. rectangular tissues, df.tps specifies degrees freedom along length, degrees freedom along width calculated ceiling(width / length * df.tps). Similarly, lambda.parameter specifies smoothing parameter regularizing regression coefficients. 1 value provided, specifies lambda.biology library size functions. 2 values provided, first value specifies lambda.biology second value specifies lambda.library size. Batch effects regularised. counts assay DelayedArray (e.g. disk-backed via HDF5Array), normalisation step automatically performed block-wise full matrix never realised memory ; results identical -memory path. block size follows DelayedArray's global auto block size, can tuned DelayedArray::setAutoBlockSize(). Batch effects can specified using batch parameter. parameter vector, design matrix created within function using model.matrix. custom design provided form numeric matrix, ideally created using model.matrix. batch matrix created intercept term. SpaNorm function automatically detect intercept term remove relevant column. Alternatively, users can subset model matrix remove column manually. Please note model formula include intercept term intercept column subset .

title: GLM-based (SpaNorm) PCA — SpaNormPCA
text: GLM-based PCA using SpaNorm model. null model considered consist library size effects, batch effects, gene mean. GLM-PCA approximated regressing null model data, performing PCA residuals (Pearson deviance).

title: GLM-based (SpaNorm) PCA — SpaNormPCA

what: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
title: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
text: Computes fitted mean matrix mu = exp(gmean + tcrossprod(alpha, W)) per-gene coefficients negative binomial GLM, optional per-gene winsorisation log-means median +/- winsor * MAD bound influence extreme fitted values. Exposed downstream packages (e.g. spiDE) can reconstruct fitted means fitNB result; generic (intercept-free) fit pass gmean = rep(0, nrow(alpha)).

title: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
code: calculateMu(   gmean,   alpha,   W,   winsor = DEFAULT_WINSOR,   backend = c("cpu", "auto", "gpu") )

title: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
text: gmean numeric vector per-gene intercepts (length nrow(alpha)). alpha genes x covariates matrix coefficients. W cells x covariates design matrix. winsor numeric, number MADs per-gene log-means winsorised (default 4); Inf disables winsorisation. backend one "cpu" (default – always returns base R matrix, matching function's behaviour GPU dispatch added), "gpu" "auto" (use accelerator one available; result torch tensor case, matrix).

title: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
text: genes x cells matrix (torch tensor, backend resolves accelerator) fitted means.

title: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
text: Runs accelerator backend resolves one (alpha/ W/gmean pushed onto active device via toGPUMatrix/toGPUVector); result torch tensor case (convert back toRMatrix needed), base R matrix otherwise.

title: Compute fitted means from a negative binomial GLM fit — calculateMu
code: set.seed(1) Y <- matrix(rpois(20 * 50, 5), 20, 50) W <- cbind(1, scale(seq_len(50))) fit <- fitNB(Y, W, verbose = FALSE) mu <- calculateMu(fit$gmean, fit$alpha, W) dim(mu) #> [1] 20 50

what: Fit a per-gene negative binomial GLM — fitNB
title: Fit a per-gene negative binomial GLM — fitNB
text: Fits per-gene negative binomial regression arbitrary design matrix using SpaNorm's IRLS engine (iteratively reweighted least squares per-gene dispersion estimated edgeR::estimateDisp). exposes fitting machinery independently SpaNorm's spatial model: fitted model log(mu) = W %*% t(alpha) built-intercept, encode one column W per-gene baseline required. corresponds internal fit .spanorm = FALSE (shared library-size column, ridge applied every column W).

title: Fit a per-gene negative binomial GLM — fitNB
code: fitNB(   Y,   W,   idx = rep(TRUE, ncol(Y)),   lambda.a = 0,   winsor = DEFAULT_WINSOR,   maxit.psi = 25,   maxit.nb = 50,   tol = 1e-04,   ...,   backend = c("auto", "cpu", "gpu"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fit a per-gene negative binomial GLM — fitNB
text: Y genes x cells matrix counts (dense, sparse, DelayedArray). W cells x covariates numeric design matrix. idx logical vector (length ncol(Y)) selecting cells used fit model (default: cells). lambda.numeric ridge penalty columns W: single value per-column vector (default 0, .e. unregularised). winsor numeric, number MADs per-gene coefficients winsorised fitting (default 4). Must single positive number; Inf disables winsorisation entirely. maxit.psi numeric, maximum number dispersion iterations. maxit.nb numeric, maximum number NB mean IRLS iterations. tol numeric, convergence tolerance. ... additional fitting parameters forwarded internal fitter, e.g. maxn.psi (dispersion-estimation subsample size) step.factor (IRLS step-halving factor). backend character, compute backend ('auto', 'cpu', 'gpu'). verbose logical, whether print progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Fit a per-gene negative binomial GLM — fitNB
text: list per-gene coefficients alpha (genes x covariates), dispersions psi, gmean element (always zero – generic fit intercept term), sampling factor, per-iteration loglik.

title: Fit a per-gene negative binomial GLM — fitNB
code: set.seed(1) Y <- matrix(rpois(20 * 50, 5), 20, 50) W <- cbind(1, scale(seq_len(50))) # intercept + one covariate fit <- fitNB(Y, W, verbose = FALSE) str(fit$alpha) #>  num [1:20, 1:2] 1.65 1.6 1.72 1.62 1.62 ...

what: Invert a batch of symmetric positive-definite matrices — invert_mat_batched
title: Invert a batch of symmetric positive-definite matrices — invert_mat_batched
text: Batched counterpart invert_mat: inverts (batch, n, n) stack symmetric positive-definite matrices single Cholesky factorisation call rather looping invert_mat() slice. SpaNorm's model never needs (per-gene fit shares single coefficient across genes, ever inverts one matrix time); exposed downstream packages (e.g. spiDE) need genuinely per-gene/per-feature covariance – different n x n matrix every slice batch, varying per-gene working weight – looping invert_mat()'s R-level tryCatch/Cholesky call per gene dominant cost.

title: SpaNorm 1.8.0
text: SpaNorm() gains BPPARAM argument parallelise normalisation across workers via BiocParallel, accelerating large datasets. defaults BiocParallel::SerialParam() (parallelisation), results identical regardless backend used. SpaNorm() now normalises DelayedArray-backed count assays (e.g. disk-backed via HDF5Array) block-wise, --core datasets processed without ever loading full matrix memory. Results match -memory path. Exported fitNB(), fits per-gene negative binomial GLM arbitrary design matrix using SpaNorm’s IRLS engine (optional ridge regularisation adjustable outlier winsorisation). exposes model-fitting machinery reuse independently SpaNorm’s spatial model.

title: SpaNorm 1.8.0
text: optional GPU backend now uses torch package instead TensorFlow, adding native support NVIDIA CUDA Apple Silicon (Metal/MPS) devices removing Python/reticulate dependency. Users backend = "gpu" install torch place tensorflow. GPU backend now automatically detects available accelerator memory fits large datasets gene-blocks peak GPU memory stays bounded, avoiding --memory failures GPUs limited VRAM. requires additional arguments; detected budget can overridden via new gpu.mem.budget parameter, results match CPU backend within small numerical tolerance. dispersion winsorisation used normalisation now clamps 4 MAD (previously 3), matching coefficient mean winsorisation, configurable via winsorisation controls fitting/normalisation helpers. Exported generic GPU device/memory-budget/tensor-conversion layer (checkGPU(), getBackendDevice(), getBackendDtype(), gpuDtypeBytes(), getGPUMemoryBudget(), resetGPUCache(), is_torch_tensor(), toGPUMatrix(), toGPUVector(), toRMatrix(), diag_mat(), tcrossprod_gpu(), matmul_gpu(), add_vec_mat_gpu(), mult_vec_mat_gpu(), rowSums_gpu(), dnbinom_gpu(), hasBadValues()) added new invert_mat_batched(), downstream packages (e.g. spiDE) can build GPU-blocked per-gene computation device/dtype/memory-budget machinery, without depending unexported internals. calculateMu() now accepts backend argument ("cpu" default, unchanged behaviour) dispatches accelerator requested.

title: SpaNorm 1.2.0
text: Added model-based spatially variable gene (SVG) calling. Added spatial visualisation funciton plotSpatial visualise colData, gene expression, reduced dimensions. Added spatial visualisation function plotCovariate visualise biolgy, batch, library size functions estimated SpaNorm. Dynamic calculation df.tps rectangular tissue sections. Allow separate specification df.tps biology library size. Added GLM-PCA approximation SpaNormPCA function. null model considered consist library size effects, batch effects, gene mean.
